results.rendered_html:
kauṭa a. (ṭī f.) [kūṭa-añ]
1 Living in one's own house; hence, independent, free.
2 Domestic, homely, homebred.
3 Fraudulent, dishonest.
4 Snared.
— ṭaḥ
1 Fraud, falsehood.
2 Giving false evidence.
Comp.
— jaḥ the Kuṭaja tree.
— takṣaḥ (opp. grāmatakṣaḥ) an independent carpenter, one who works at home on his own account and not for the village.
— sākṣin m. a false witness.
— sākṣyaṃ false evidence, perjury.
